observations.what_customers_say: Some reviews point to installation handover or system instruction feeling too rushed.
observations.decision_relevance: Buyers may want to know how they will learn to operate and monitor the system after commissioning.

observations.what_customers_say: Some customer language focuses on slow support or unresolved post-install issues.
observations.decision_relevance: Service reliability can become a pre-contract concern for cautious buyers.

observations.what_customers_say: Some review language focuses on property damage disputes and installation-risk concerns.
observations.decision_relevance: A buyer with roof or building concerns may need specific responsibility, documentation, and warranty details.

observations.what_customers_say: Positive review language also praises punctual and clean installation teams.
observations.decision_relevance: Installation-day proof can matter for customers concerned about disruption at home.

observations.what_customers_say: Some reviews mention delays or friction around metering and registration steps.
observations.decision_relevance: The customer may need clarity on what happens after panels are mounted but before the system is fully usable.

observations.what_customers_say: The source contains both praise for field teams and complaints about damage or support.
observations.decision_relevance: The useful pattern is not a verdict on one company, but the categories customers care about after signing.

cross_source_notes: Roof/property concerns also appear in public forum discussions.
Handover and support concerns overlap with broader post-install trust patterns.
caveats: The source mixes praise and complaints.
Property-damage concerns should be handled with installer-specific facts, not copied from this provider.
